control_description: The entity authorizes, designs, develops or acquires, configures, documents, tests, approves, and implements changes to infrastructure, data, software, and procedures to meet its objectives.
prompt: Review the change management policy for Orion Health and assess whether it adequately addresses SOC 2 CC8.1 requirements. Identify any gaps or missing elements.

findings.title: No segregation of duties in change deployment
findings.description: Section 4.3 states 'Changes are deployed by the requesting engineer.' The same person who requests a change also deploys it. CC8.1 requires segregation of duties — the person who develops/requests a change should not be the same person who approves or deploys it to production.
findings.expected_reasoning: The requesting engineer deploys their own changes. No independent reviewer or deployer is required. This eliminates a key control — segregation between development and deployment.

findings.title: No testing requirement before production deployment
findings.description: The policy mentions CI/CD pipeline goes from staging to production but does not require testing in staging, test results sign-off, or QA validation before promotion to production. CC8.1 explicitly requires that changes are 'tested' before implementation.
findings.expected_reasoning: Section 4.3 mentions staging but has no testing gate. Changes could be pushed from staging to production without any test execution or validation.

findings.title: Emergency change process lacks controls
findings.description: Emergency changes require only verbal approval and a retroactive Jira ticket. No requirement for post-implementation security review, no time limit for creating the retroactive ticket, and no verification that the emergency change didn't introduce vulnerabilities.
findings.expected_reasoning: Verbal approval is not auditable. Retroactive documentation could be created long after the change. No security review of emergency changes.
